formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a comprehensive formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Computer Science - Computer Systems in JSON format:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Amdahl's Law",
    "formula": "S = 1 / (1 - p + p/n)",
    "variables": "S - maximum theoretical speedup, p - percentage of program that can be parallelized, n - number of processors",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Amdahl's Law calculates the maximum theoretical speedup that can be achieved by parallel processing."
  },
  {
    "name": "Gustafson's Law",
    "formula": "S = p \* n",
    "variables": "S - speedup, p - proportion of program that can be parallelized, n - number of processors",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Gustafson's Law states that the speedup of parallel processing is proportional to the number of processors."
  },
  {
    "name": "Little's Law",
    "formula": "L = λW",
    "variables": "L - average number of jobs in the system, λ - arrival rate of jobs, W - average response time",
    "units": "L - (jobs), λ - (jobs/time), W - (time)",
    "notes": "Little's Law relates the average number of jobs in a system to the arrival rate and average response time."
  },
  {
    "name": "CPU Utilization",
    "formula": "CPU Utilization = (CPU Time / Total Time) * 100",
    "variables": "CPU Utilization - percentage of CPU time used, CPU Time - time spent by CPU in executing instructions, Total Time - total time elapsed",
    "units": "%",
    "notes": "CPU Utilization measures the percentage of time spent by CPU in executing instructions."
  },
  {
    "name": "Throughput",
    "formula": "Throughput = Number of tasks completed / Total time elapsed",
    "variables": "Throughput - number of tasks completed per unit time, Number of tasks completed - number of tasks completed, Total time elapsed - total time elapsed",
    "units": "(tasks/time)",
    "notes": "Throughput measures the number of tasks completed per unit time."
  },
  {
    "name": "Response Time",
    "formula": "Response Time = Time taken to complete a task",
    "variables": "Response Time - time taken to complete a task, Time taken to complete a task - time taken to complete a task",
    "units": "(time)",
    "notes": "Response Time measures the time taken to complete a task."
  },
  {
    "name": "First-Come-First-Served (FCFS) Scheduling",
    "formula": "FCFS = (arrival time, service time)",
    "variables": "FCFS - First-Come-First-Served scheduling, arrival time - time at which a job arrives, service time - time taken to complete a job",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "FCFS scheduling serves jobs in the order of their arrival."
  },
  {
    "name": "Shortest Job First (SJF) Scheduling",
    "formula": "SJF = (service time, waiting time)",
    "variables": "SJF - Shortest Job First scheduling, service time - time taken to complete a job, waiting time - time spent waiting for service",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "SJF scheduling serves the job with the shortest service time first."
  },
  {
    "name": "Round-Robin Scheduling",
    "formula": "Time Quantum = (Time Quanta / Number of Processes)",
    "variables": "Time Quantum - time allocated to each process, Time Quanta - total time allowed for each process, Number of Processes - number of processes in the system",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Round-Robin Scheduling allocates a fixed time quantum to each process."
  }
]
```

This JSON array includes key formulas and laws related to Computer Systems, such as Amdahl's Law, Gustafson's Law, Little's Law, CPU Utilization, Throughput, Response Time, First-Come-First-Served (FCFS) Scheduling, Shortest Job First (SJF) Scheduling, and Round-Robin Scheduling. Each formula is accompanied by a description of the variables, units, and notes to help students understand the concept.
